test.command: PYTEST_ADDOPTS='-p no:pytest_ethereum' BROWNIE_NETWORK=mainnet .venv/bin/pytest tests/prices/test_convex.py tests/test_cachebox.py -W ignore -s --tb=short -v
test.notes: 11 convex-specific tests passed. All 15 cachebox tests passed (including test_no_unbounded_memory_caches). A blocking issue was found in the initial review: cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize on separate lines in y/prices/convex.py, violating the cachebox bounds test. Fixed in commit 86cf701c before writing synthesis. Full suite prior run (test_full_convex.log) showed 279 failures but these are all pre-existing concurrent-test-execution failures documented in baseline-green.md.

typecheck.notes: mypy reports 1687 errors in 83 files but all are pre-existing codebase errors consistent with prior milestone scrutiny. No new errors introduced by convex.py.

lint.command: N/A - ruff and flake8 not installed in project venv
lint.notes: No linter installed. Code follows existing project conventions.

fixesApplied.description: cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize on separate decorator lines in y/prices/convex.py (lines 52 and 97). The test_no_unbounded_memory_caches test scans source files line-by-line and requires both parameters to appear on the same line. This caused the cachebox bounds test to fail.
fixesApplied.fix: Consolidated cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize onto the same line in both @a_sync.a_sync decorators. Fixed in commit 86cf701c.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: When using @a_sync.a_sync with cache_type='memory', always put cache_type and ram_cache_maxsize on the SAME line. The test_no_unbounded_memory_caches test scans source files line-by-line and fails if they appear on separate lines. Pattern: `cache_type="memory", ram_cache_maxsize=ENVS.DEFAULT_CACHE_MAXSIZE,`
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This same issue appeared in erc4626 (y/prices/erc4626.py, fixed in f246ab0d) and convex (y/prices/convex.py, fixed in 86cf701c). Both workers independently made the same mistake of putting them on separate lines.
